explanations.3: This tells the system to skip interactive prompts during installation, so the Docker build won't get stuck waiting for user input.
explanations.7: The `-q` flag makes wget run quietly without printing download progress, keeping the Docker build output cleaner.
explanations.9: After installing Chrome, we delete the apt package cache to reduce the final Docker image size, since we won't need those files again.
explanations.11: The `--disable-dev-shm-usage` flag tells Chrome to use disk instead of shared memory (which might be limited in containers), and `--disable-gpu` turns off GPU acceleration since containers often can't access the host's graphics hardware.
